citations.narrative:
An unguarded electrical cord for a heating element was within the outdoor primary enclosure for 2 adult ring-tail lemurs.
The cord was plugged into an extension cord but not plugged into the wall at the time of inspection. Electrical cords within
the primary enclosure may result in adverse events such as electrocution, entanglement, or strangulation. Primary
enclosures must be constructed and maintained so that they protect the nonhuman primates from injury.
Correct by facility at the time of inspection April 1, 2026.

The USDA inspector noted 2 issues with the domestic goat enclosures.
-During the inspection 2 juvenile goats exited the enclosure going through the wire panel. The inspector measured the
opening that the goats used at 6 inches X 8 inches. The fence in this area was insufficient to contain the animals securely.
-There was a supplemental area of fencing that had been applied to the enclosure. This supplemental fencing had
become damaged and bent. This led to singular wires pointing into the animal space at the level of the goats. The fence
not being maintained in good repair allowed for sharp points that can present a potential for injury.
The facilities must be constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved. The
outdoor housing facilities shall be structurally sound and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the animals from
injury and to contain the animals.
Correct by April 16, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.80(a)(2)(ii) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
An unguarded electrical cord for a heating element was within the outdoor primary enclosure for 2 adult ring-tail lemurs. 
The cord was plugged into an extension cord but not plugged into the wall at the time of inspection. Electrical cords within 
the primary enclosure may result in adverse events such as electrocution, entanglement, or strangulation. Primary 
enclosures must be constructed and maintained so that they protect the nonhuman primates from injury. 
 
Correct by facility at the time of inspection April 1, 2026. 
3.125(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
The USDA inspector noted 2 issues with the domestic goat enclosures.  
 
-During the inspection 2 juvenile goats exited the enclosure going through the wire panel. The inspector measured the 
opening that the goats used at 6 inches X 8 inches. The fence in this area was insufficient to contain the animals securely.  
 
-There was a supplemental area of fencing that had been applied to the enclosure. This supplemental fencing had 
become damaged and bent. This led to singular wires pointing into the animal space at the level of the goats. The fence 
not being maintained in good repair allowed for sharp points that can present a potential for injury.  
 
The facilities must be constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved. The 
outdoor housing facilities shall be structurally sound and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the animals from 
injury and to contain the animals. 
 
Correct by April 16, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
